C64 Serial Registry
Thanks for visiting the Commodore 64 serial registry.

This page is dedicated to keeping track of the C64's that are still alive and well and in the hands of collectors around the world. Perhaps we can find who has the earliest and the latest serial numbers once and for all?

This data is collected on the 'honor system' so please don't enter false or known incorrct information- that helps nobody!
Also, please do not attempt to enter modern reproduction serial numbers from new boards!

Serial: 0019
Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: 6 Owner: Unknown Location: USA
Notes: Prototype auctioned at VCF 2003. Power light label says "POWER" and not "64" like the first production runs. Serial number is a typed paper label, but matches the one for #21 that was submitted. Ths unit belonged to Charles Winterble, according to the auction.

Board Version: KU-14194HB Owner: Federico Location: Italy

Notes: Two switches were added - one for reset, and one to enable/disable the cartridge. I believe this was originally owned by a school as it has a number engraved on the bottom of the case. Owner: unknown Location: unknown
Notes: Facebook auction 2019-05-26
Serial: S00007845
Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: REV C Owner: JC Hodgson Location: United States WA
Notes: The cartridge shield has (blurry, hard to read): CBM OA- 0 AOC The video unit cover has (again, blurry): CBM OA- 15 ACC The video unit cover also has a sticker: USI MT P/N:326130-01 MADE IN TAIWAN R.O.C. I bought this new in 1982 at a trade show in Spokane WA USA. It's had only moderate use, and very little for the past 20+ years. Never had any service done, but now it has problems: instead of the blue startup screen, the startup is a sickly green shade and the various colors don't display correctly, and cartridge games have problems with the display. Hope I can find a good home for it, with someone who can fix it. One of my favorite memories: when my older daughter (born in 1982) was 2 or 3 years old she loved to sit on my lap and play "Kids on Keys"; she would giggle and giggle as the falling letters were popped.

Serial: s00008687
Board Version: 3262398 Board Revision: c Board Serial: n/a Owner: Kenneth Bell Location: Texas,usa
Notes: white ic sockets on 6510 and VIC chip. ceramic VIC. very clean running unit
Serial: S00008813
Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: C Owner: John C. Lonningdal Location: Norway
Notes: Could not find Board Serial or Country anywhere. Sticker on RF modulator says USI MT P/N: 326130-01, Made in Taiwan R.O.C. VIC-II is ceramic (MOS 6567T2 2982), RAM chips are also ceramic (M5K4164NS-20 JAPAN 82091A, last number is different on each chip). Kernal Rom is also ceramic (MOS 901227-01 1982 CBM 3182). CPU is not ceramic MOS 6510 1882). SID is not ceramic (MOS 6581 2282). Character rom is not ceramic (MOS 901225-01 1982 CBM 1982). Basic rom is not ceramic (MOS 901226-01 1982 CBM 2482). CIA chip is not ceramic (MOS 6526 1982). Chip next to SID has a white sticker saying "C64 REV2 7E17).
Serial: S00008964
Board Version: Unknown Board Revision: Unknown Board Serial: Unknown Owner: Mike Sadowski Location: Northlake, IL
Notes: Purchased online at LiveAuctioneers.com. This is the listing: https://www.liveauctioneers.com/item/209277037_commodore-64-system-with-power-supply-vic-1541-mp-801-and-manuals-gurnee-il - Picked it up in person after winning since it was local to my area. Paid $150.00 for it. Silver badge with "64" next to power light. Came with a power supply, newer 1541 disk drive, an older VIC1541 disk drive (in foam packing only, no outer sleeve), MPS801 printer (with beat-up box), data cables, video cable, and 2 books. C64 works but did not test the accessories. Could not find Board Serial or Country anywhere. Sticker on RF modulator says USI MT P/N: 326130-01, Made in Taiwan R.O.C. Did not look at the VIC-II chip. RAM chips are not ceramic (MSM3764-20RS 24092). Kernal Rom is ceramic, numbers are worn off the top so I cannot read them. CPU is not ceramic MOS 6510 2882). SID is not ceramic (MOS 6581 2282). Character rom is not ceramic (MOS 901225-01 1982 CBM 1982). Basic ROM is not ceramic (MOS 901226-01 (C) 1982 CBM 2682). CIA chip is not ceramic (MOS 6526 2182). Chip next to SID has a white sticker saying "C64 REV2 7E17").

Notes: Original Kernal ROM has been replaced due to failure. All RAM chips replaced due to failure. Serial: S00028571
Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: A Board Serial: 326116 (03401 label) Owner: Conrad Vogel Location: North Idaho
Notes: Absolute Mint, zero marks, Never have i seen one nicer. I am the 2nd owner. 1st owner used for a year and stored until now. He supplied original sales receipt from Dayton's in MN, dated 03/24/83. He got a 10% discount because his new wife worked there. Original Gold Ceramic RAM dated 8215. Ceramic VIC-II dated 4182. SID 2382, other chips similar. PLA only non-original chip replaced 8411 (REV3 paper sticker). Original Box w/ matching S/N. Original 1st printing User's Guide & Programmer's Ref Guide.

Serial: WGB20028
Board Version: KU-14194HB Board Revision: REV.B Owner: Michael Carey Location: South Australia
Notes: This was bought new in 1983 and is still operational. Original ceramic VICII, SID and CPU. CIA U2 has been replaced in the past due to wayward experiments with the User Port. This machine needed minor PCB repairs due to electrolyte corrosion. It has had a full capacitor replacement and is now fitted with a SKS Kernal Switcher. Sadly the original Silver Sticker was ripped off by a younger naive me :-(

Serial: WGC1023
Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: A Board Serial: 04365 Owner: Patod75 Location: Italy
Notes: Bought as "spare parts" at a ridiculous price. Fully restored. Has its "buggy board" with original chipset (datecodes range from 18/82 to 49/82), Fairchild PLA and ceramic R1 VIC. Factory fashion poor video signal out, no reset mod, cardboard present, top case's fragile hooks almost intact. "Replica" Silver Labels installed, had Rainbow ones when I found it. Fully functional (April 2021).

Notes: This 64 came with a repairable power supply. Remove the screws and open it up. I had to repair it once. Some keys became intermittent. I replaced the key switches with a surplus C-16 keyboard. I kept the 64 back and used the C-16 keys. I bought it in early 1983. Keyboard sticker date is 82-12-14.

Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: REV.A Board Serial: 326116 Owner: David Smith Location: United States
Serial: S00098647
Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: REV A Board Serial: 326116 Owner: Ron Jackson Location: New York State
Notes: Board is an original silver label board with chips manufactured in 1982 except the PLA which was replaced with REV 3 in 1984. It has the brass cover for the back of the board and the cartridge slot is green. The power button however is black not green on the inside and the case has the rainbow logo. Best guess this C64 was either an early rainbow model which included parts from the original silver label, or it was a repair and the techs swapped out the silver label cover with a new one. System runs fine however it does need direct luma - chroma video connection on a 1701 or 1702 monitor to produce the best image without color artifacts.

Notes: I am unsure about the board S/N: Stamp in cartridge shield says: "326166 (K.H.)", a "3" written manually on it. A label covered the whole stamp, and it says: "C64B3 P HKC S/N 371833 WW /86/87" with "BNE" written manually in the first line. Additionally, a QC sticker on the shield says: "Q C ASSEMBLED IN HONG KONG PASS" Moreover, a sticker on the keyboard's bottom side says: "326166-03" (pre-printed) and "17861228" (print added). I can get no sound out of the monitor port, and cannot try the RF port.

Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: REV. 3 Board Serial: 027030 Owner: Arjan Kuiper Location: Oude Pekela, The Netherlands
Notes: This is a genuine "Aldi" model. When opened up it reveals the following information: Keyboard sticker with the following information: 326166-03, 20870204. On the board it says PCB ASSY NO.250469, PCB NO.252311 REV 3, WV01 8712. There are stickers on the RF modulator with the following information: VDE, P/N 251916-02. Also there are 3 smaller stickers on the cartridge port with the following information: UAL 14 Passed, FINAL Q.C PASSED, BURN IN. And one larger sticker on the cartridge port which includes the board serial, wich says the following: C64E P, UAL S/N 027030, WW 16/87/88. There's also 326116CS stamped on the cartridge port shield.

Notes: This is a C64 "Aldi" bread bin. Just a small amount of these units were produced in 1987, making it a rare model. Mint condition! Considering the rarity, it's very likely there might be just a few units in this overall condition.

Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: REV.B Board Serial: 2235675 Owner: Wertzui Location: Hungary
Notes: Got it from a local "junk shop" around 2008 or 2009. I guess they imported it from a German garbage deposit. It came with a 1541 II disk drive and a C64GS cartridge. Warranty seal was already broken over the screwhole. It worked well, until 2010 when it was flooded with water. What a shame. The case is still in good condition tough.

Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: A Board Serial: 98831 Owner: Jim Happel Location: Near Chicago
Notes: My C64 from when I was 14! Graphics on the front of the O key are worn off from too much Omega Race. I guess I had the "buggy" board? LOL, got me though engineering school, college papers, school logins (80 column Kermit was pretty terrible to read but worked) etc... not to mention lots of games with no real apparent bugs. This year I got it out of the attic after probably 20 years. Got re-hooked on it. Wow, the 5 pin video is terrible. Was it always this bad? Bought a 64c off ebay so I could have decent S-video out.

Notes: Purchased untested at auction, and was working when I received it. I recapped it, added some heat sinks, replaced R10 with 120ohm and did the restore key mod. I intend to sell this one. Serial: P00399574
Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: A Board Serial: 159684 Owner: Will A Location: Nashville, TN
Notes: "12 3 12 B" or "A 3 A B" in permanent marker bottom-L of board; 5-pin video out; R36 appears clipped off at the board (computer still fully operational); when I received it the #1 and #2 key were not functional (but were cleaned and repaired); I performed a complete tear-down, cleaning, and re-capping of all electrolytic capacitors.

Board Version: 250407 Board Revision: A Board Serial: Unknown Owner: Mike Sadowski Location: Northlake, IL
Notes: Purchased at an estate sale in Waukegan, IL. Works, but needs to be cleaned and retrobrighted. Came with power supply, supporting manuals, books, data and TV cables. Numbers on bottom of power supply say 15-83 (15th week of 1983?). Came with serial number matching box, but box is missing cardboard insert for cables, does include foam inserts. Came with VIC-1541 floppy drive with games and software on floppies in original box. Floppy drive box has price sticker from a store called "Child World Children's Palace". Unknown if purchased at same time as computer, but probably from the Waukegan, IL location? Silver cardboard RF shield sits between motherboard and keyboard.

Serial: P00934032
Owner: Mike O'neill Location: Cromwell, CT, USA
Notes: 8pin and six pin in the back of the unit. I beleive this is a original as it was found in it original rotting box which was saved by the plastic around the board. I aquired this unit "YES" by trading an older Craftsman tool box for it; as i have many a tool boxes for trading something different. I do not remember much about the commodore as i was young when they were first released and eventually centered my attention to the macintosh IIC. I do alot of trading for 90% of my systems / games for these commodores and all my other gaming desires. I will be posting pics of these serials i enter here within the next few months on my facebook page facebook/mnjmetals

Serial: p00988592
Board Version: 25Q407 Board Revision: B Board Serial: 485243 Owner: Timothy C.B. Location: La
Notes: Was mint before it got shipped to me. shipping droped it wich caused the keyboard to brake the 3key post and all and broke case plastics shoving keyboard into the unit. nearly every standoff or conective post in the plastics is snaped external plastic damige on top case where each post was attaching the keyboard. case screws are snaped all posts but one motherboard atachments are snaped away from case. one crack in bottem of case near power port. plastics are brittle and i'm considering rebuilding the case. Motherboard is in clean condition few caps were shoved sideways when keyboard was shoved into case. one cap had a leg ripped free and was replaced. board is working fine now. 6 pin din for video

Serial: P2243811
Board Version: Unknown Board Revision: Unknown Board Serial: Unknown Owner: Mike Sadowski Location: Northlake, IL
Notes: Purchased at a "consignment" garage sale in Huntley, IL 8/17/2019. All price tags said they belonged to "Jim". Works, but needs to be cleaned and (the top cover mostly) needs to be retrobrighted. Came with power supply, manual, a book, a magazine, a plastic keyboard dust cover, the Incredible Musical keyboard (complete in box minus bottom part of box), an OKIMATE 10 color printer (complete in boxes), a 1541 disk drive (complete in box minus data cable), some programs and games on disks and cartridges (most complete in box), a broken light pen, TV cable and Commodore RF adapter. Numbers on bottom of power supply says 0584 (5th week of 1984?). Came with serial number matching box (complete). Printer box has price sticker from Target. Replacement ribbon for printer came from a store called "Zayre". Silver cardboard RF shield sits between motherboard and keyboard, but looks like someone soldered the cardboard RF shield to the cartridge port with a piece of copper and stuck a piece of electrical tape to it. Did not want to remove this to look at motherboard since the computer worked.
Serial: P2248092

Board Version: 250425 Board Serial: 139442 Owner: Zachary Folkerts Location: Alabama, USA
Notes: This machine was given to me by a friend from Tennessee: it was sitting in his garage for years, so there was a lot of rust on RF output enclosure, cartridge port, and metal shield. It came with a tattered box, but the serial no. listed there is: P2386988. I steel-wooled most of the rust away and am using it free of moving parts with 64hdd, an X1541 cable, and an IBM ThinkPad T22 (Parallel port in Bi-Directional mode) running from CompactFlash.

Notes: The system is painted Gold. Lots of information about the Gold system in Germany exists, but I can't find any information on the Gold systems in the US. If it was painted post production, somebody did an outstanding job. It's painted all the way through, but after opening it, it didn't look like it had ever been opened. old crusty tape still holding the ground strapping to the cardboard shield.

Notes: Received this and another C64 off craigslist. System was really dirty and disgusting with missing broken keys. Used both keyboards to make a complete working one. Notes: original board FAILED & recycled for parts was assembly 250425 - 1984 Kalex, Commodore INTL, 94V-0, 28125, Made in Hong Kong. Replacement board installed is: assembly 250407 1983 Commodore INTL, s/n 31738, Assembled in Hong Kong.
Serial: P5646858B
Board Version: 250425 Board Serial: 326116 Owner: Ferris Whitney Location: Sarasota, Florida
Serial: P5648201B
Board Version: Unknown Board Revision: B Board Serial: Unknown Owner: Mike Sadowski Location: Northlake, IL
Notes: Purchased at a flea market that was in Fox Lake, IL. Didn't work when purchased (probably why it was at the flea market in the first place). Found someone in Hoffman Estates, IL who repairs Commodores. He diagnosed that one of the memory chips was bad. We traded motherboards with a spare one that he had that worked and later told me he replaced the bad memory chip in my board and got it to work. He said that other memory chips had been changed in the past by the previous owner, as the printing on the chips didn't match each other. Came with a Datasette drive, power supply and supporting manuals and books.

Notes: Main ICs date between 26th week and 33rd week of 1984, PLA replaced with a 1986 chip. All main ICs in original sockets. VIC-II is a 6567R8. 8 MT 4264-20 RAM chips. Most of the keys are smooth on top from use. Overall condition of the computer from 1-10 is 8.

Serial: RP0000584
Board Version: ??? Owner: redeyelou Location: Durham, NC
Notes: Mysterious red button under logo / above keyboard. C64 in box with matching serials. Official refurb sticker on box. Unable to open casing without risk of damage due to red button installation. No board info on edge under keyboard (can peek in a bit, took pics). Untested while identifying...
Serial: RP0000850
Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: A Board Serial: 331757 Owner: Keri Rautenkranz Location: Chicago area
Notes: I purchased this refurbished C64 in the mid '80s, did not like it, and gave it to my father who ran his business with it for 10 years. I have recently returned it to service after a 25-year hiatus with the intent of recovering Dad's data. To this end, I rebuilt the power supply sections and corrected the famous flaws endemic to this board, which now works better than ever. 5182 dated SID works great, having never been used for gaming.

Board Version: 250407 Board Revision: B Board Serial: 874614 Owner: Matthew Lange Location: Ottawa, ON, Canada
Notes: Bought off eBay in ~2007 for parts. Was missing SID chip and two other ICs, the 'B' and ':' keys were missing, the power LED had been replaced by a previous owner. The case had been held together for what must have been a number of years with opaque brown packing tape, which degraded. I salved the case with a lot of hard work, and am using it for a parts unit.

Notes: Integrated Circuit 'U17 / 82S100 PLA / C 906114-01' below the CPU has a white label with the following info: C64 REV2 7E17 Does this indicate that the PLA chip was updated after the initial build? This is my original C64 purchased in March 1983, but cannot recall if it was ever serviced at Commodore in Toronto for any reason. I have hi-res photos available of this C64 if you would like them for any reason. Thanks, Mark

Notes: Bought this along with a 1541 from ebay auction. Auction had no pictures of serial number or board or rear of machine. Nobody bidding knew this was a 5 pin video with green cartridge port. Serial: UKB827559
Owner: Petri Remesaho Location: Finland
Serial: UKB827755
Board Version: 250407 Board Revision: B Board Serial: 987193 Owner: Jussi Saily Location: Finland
Notes: This machine is my first computer which my daddy bought me in 1983. It was retired in the toolshed in mid 90s due to a bad VIC. I revived it in 2008 with a new VIC, PLA & SID (there was no sound). Later 2008 some glue logic ICs failed, and I did a complete overhaul with new (NOS!) chips: CPU, VIC, memory chips, all glue logic, one CIA, new electrolytic caps. I expect it to run another 25 years now ;-) It's running JiffyDOS now too.

Board Version: 250425 Board Revision: A Board Serial: 079634 Owner: DigitalH Location: Italy
Notes: Very good conditions, works perfectly. All components are original except: VIC-II replaced (original doesn't work) and all electrolytic capacitors replaced with new ones. VIC-II, SID, PLA and CPU heatsinked. All chips are XX84. 8 pin video. VIC-II, MOS 8701, SID, PLA, CPU, KERNAL ROM, BASIC ROM, CHAR ROM and two CIAs are socketed. Mitsumi modulator.
Serial: UKB1653322
Board Version: 250407 Board Revision: REV.B Board Serial: 1041050 Owner: Davide Giazzi Location: Italy
Notes: Purchased 09/2019 from ebay. Don't know if the seller was the first owner. The original box is not in good condition. It works. No ceramic chip on board and they are all soldered except VIC, Kernal and SID. They are all dated 1984. The case is a little yellowed and the power supply seams to have overheated. I've applied new clips at the top cover cause they were all broken and added SIDFX with the original 6581 and a 8580R5 (1989) and of course I replaced the power supply.

Serial: UKB2359291
Board Version: 250407 Board Revision: REV.B Board Serial: ? Owner: Jonathan Bishop Location: UK BUCKINGHAMSHIRE
Notes: Board Serial/Batch Number. There is no sticker or printed number on the Cartridge Sheild. However, there is two sticker's, on the RF/CH Screw Component. One on the top, and one on the side. Top sticker has three signatures on it, "Heather, Jan, Sue". Side sticker reads, "MD6-UE3603 532 T", with a black bold circle at the end of the T. There is no sticker or print, that identifies where the board was made. The only other sticker I could find, was 4 inches to the left of the assembly number, which reads, "DE 4004208". There is also a number printed onto the the large shiny metal square with tiny holes on, which is, "251024". Oh, and it's just broken down. Power light is on, screen is black, with a very low hum. Do You know anyone who could repair it for Me. Thankyou. Jon. 07721656104.

Serial: WGC0359
Board Version: 326298 Board Revision: REV A Board Serial: 03678 Owner: Marco Location: Netherlands
Notes: I bought this c64, 15 march 2009, from its first owner. Its the cleanest c64 I've ever seen. Is has been rarely used and was always packed in its original box. The c64 looks as if it has left the factory yesterday. The man told me that he pre-ordered the c-64 so it must have been a c-64 from the first batch delevired in The Netherlands. The box carries a sticker with the serial number that on the c64 too, WGC00359 (extra zero). Other specifications: CIA: MOS 6526 3382 (2x), not ceramic -- BASIC ROM: MOS 901226-01 227C, not ceramic -- KERNAL ROM: MOS 901227-02 1982 4482 ceramic -- CHAR ROM: MOS 901225-01 1982 4482, not ceramic -- CPU: MOS 6510 1582, not ceramic -- PLA: 93459 PC / F8235 / SINGAPORE, ceramic, white sticker with c64 written on it -- SID: 6581 2282, not ceramic -- VIC II: 6569R1 4282, not ceramic

Notes: Purchased from Compute or Play store (from West Edmonton Mall) at a Commodore Computer show I believe in the summer of 1987. It was my second C64.
Serial: CA1013501C
Board Version: 250425 Board Revision: No rev. number listed Board Serial: s/n 493958 label or Stamped 326116S Owner: TimO Location: Chicago IL. USA
Notes: I am the original owner of this C64c computer. I bought it in 1986. I was surprised to find a 1984 board inside, confirmed by the assembly number and 1984 Commodore Intl. Commodore 64 printed on the board. It has the 8 pin din connector for video as well. Not sure how an 84 board would have that? I assume that the board was a left over batch being used in the 86 production line? The board did not have a rev. number printed or label on the board.

Notes: Board lacks a rev number or anything declaring country of origin on it. The board does have "OPC" followed by "94VO" and "1298A30-86" stamped on it under the heat spreader, along with a sticker declaring that this c64 was "assembled by Bremen". Garage sale find that'd been sitting for 30 years, fired right up. Appears to have a kernel Rom adapter hardwired in. Vancouver commodore club here we come :)

Notes: Purchased from a vendor at VCFMW 2019 in Elmhurst, IL. Bare unit only with beige power supply. Power supply has numbers "0988" possibly indicating it was manufactured 9th week of 1988? Power supply does not work, supplies insufficient power to turn on machine but is only able to light up power LED. Machine itself works perfectly with different power supply. Very dusty and dirty inside - metal RF shield has a lot of rust on it. Case plastic and keyboard keys are severely yellowed - needs to be retrobrighted.

Serial: HB4633262E
Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: 3 Board Serial: 074756 Owner: Stefan Location: Zeeland, Netherlands
Notes: Also on the board are the following texts/markings : Top-left : KALEX C 94V-0 K 368 20117 Bottom right : W-2594V-0 The cartrdige port shield actually has several markings/stickers. Standard stuff like BURN IN and FINAL Q.C. PASSED but a little less common is UAL 7 PASSED. The two bigger stickers read : First one : C64E P HKC S/N 679107 WW 9 /87/88 (the 9 is hand written and the 87 is strikethrough with pen) Second one : C64E UAL S/N 074756 WW 09 /87/88

Serial: HB4886512E
Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: REV.4 Board Serial: 1020966 Owner: pyksy Location: Finland
Serial: HB4899922E
Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: Rev. 4 Board Serial: 1059706 Owner: Ville Saarelainen Location: Finland
Notes: Bought from auction in April 2021, one key was missing and plunger broken, keys and case moderately yellowed. I have replaced the missing key, recapped the m/b, socketed all chips, removed the cardboard RF shield and installed heatsinks into VIC-II, SID and CPU. Chip date codes are mostly around 3588. The RF modulator had intermittent signal loss and I replaced it by "mbarszcz-pcb" composite/S-video modulator. Will be retrobrighted later.

Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: 3 Board Serial: 158689 Owner: Katarina Glantz Location: Sweden
Notes: I just have two Commodores to compare between, but this one has a sturdy metallic heat shield, which is clearly different from the flimsy silver-paper wrapping which my other C64C computer has (made in West Germany). Then there is also the color of the board, which is a beautiful green, while the other C64C is mostly golden in color. Also, at the bottom left corner of the board, someone has scribbled a number (7) similarly to the other board (2), signifying something from the assembly of the computer?

Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: REV.B Board Serial: 2098769 Owner: V-12/Tropyx Location: Szczecin, Poland
Notes: Bought by my father on 16.03.1993 in local computer store, exactly at Plac Lotnikow in Szczecin, Poland. I have original box but strangely it has different serial number (HB4 621681 E) than is printed on C64 sticker. Seems that my C64 was put to different box in the shop or something else. I'm working on this machine since the day of buy. Started to learn Basic Language in 1994. In 1995 I've founded Tropyx group. 99% of my job comes from this C64. I did some modifications inside, for example I've added second expansion port.

Notes: U11 4464 DRAM replaced to restore to working condition. U4 ROM removed and socket fitted for switchable customised JiffyDOS EPROM. EXROM/GAME toggle switch added. 6526's dated 0588. CPU/VIC-II/SID have custom heatsinks added. No top case half. Currently in use. Keyboard same as newer C64C keyboard but font is bolder than the one shown on this page.

Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: REV.4 Board Serial: 1051424 Owner: Joe Location: New Zealand
Notes: Purchased new in 1989. After SID failed in my first C64 Bread Bin. Removed from very damp half buried in garden shed storage July 2014 CPU failed at power up (still had mud inside) cleaned replaced CPU with older CPU 6510 dated 1986 works great. PCB in great condition no corrosion from years of damp. yellowed case in parts (that weren't buried) Badge de-laminated and peeling. 1531 Datasette with adapter (as supplied with original bread bin) 1541-II Disk Drive 1802 Monitor

Serial: hb4909091e
Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: REV 4 Board Serial: HKC 644023 Owner: Ant Howard Location: Australia
Notes: Purchased by me in 1988 at Target, Chadstone Shopping Centre. Repaired by High Technology Computers in ~1990 for dead motherboard. Older board inserted into case (250407) by shop tech's. Service sticker applied to case. Key board mount and power LED lead "Jerry built" by shop lay unknown untill new motherboard needed in 2012. Current (correct) board type installed in 2012 due to general failure. Mated to a "Blue Chip" DD also purchased by me in 1989. System remains in current use and enjoyment through a 1084 monitor procured in 1994.

Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: B Board Serial: 2326852 Owner: Sven Petersen Location: BruckmÃ¼hl/Germany
Notes: The serial number was blank in the middle. 987654 is just a replacement. A blank S/N usually means, that the C64 was refurbished by Commodore and sold again. The Date Codes on the MOS chips are 25-29/91. The board was labeled WW31/91. The C64 is fully functional.

Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: Rev 4 Board Serial: 1404296 Owner: Giuseppe_Giu Location: Italy
Notes: This is really weird. The case had its warranty label still intact, but inside it has signs of some repairing job. Like CPU, SID have some traces of thermal paste on it, even though it has a paper cover and not a metallic shield, so it seems they've been replaced. On the solder side there are soldering signs at the Ram chps, so the also have been replaced. I suppose it's sort of a refurbished C64 !!

Serial: HB5236482E
Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: REV. 3 Board Serial: 259926 Owner: Arjan Kuiper Location: Oude Pekela, The Netherlands
Notes: This system was bought for â‚¬10,-, which was including a matching 1541C floppy drive. The system was untested and also quite rough. 4 traces and 2 pads were almost gone, thus it was a wonder and a surprise it still worked. But as always, something had to be done to make it reliable again, thus i replaced the damaged traces and pads with some wiring. The board has also been recapped, thus welcoming it reliably into the 21st century. Also the case badge has been replaced with an upscaled "brushed metal" aftermarket badge with black lettering, since the original badge was beyond saving.

Owner: Georg Bottenhofer Location: Kranzberg, Germany

Serial: HB5321431E
Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: 4 Board Serial: 833620 Owner: Dean Maessen Location: Gold Coast, Australia
Notes: Modded keyboard combining keys from a dead original C64 and keys from the C64C. Function and system keys plus spacebar in original keys, numbers and letters in C64C keys. Also modded with 4 pots on top of the keyboard and a couple of switches to turn on the in-built paddle pots (for use with Prophet64 SID music cartridge). Used monthly or so for SID music jams and playing oldskool games.

Serial: CA1151191
Owner: Todd A Davis Location: Middleburg OH
Notes: I just booted it up after 20 years. The Keyboard connector must have been corroded at first. At first it woould only type certain characters, and would always put a "U" after each character it would type. I reseated the Keyboard connector several times and that cleared up. Now, other than not having a joystick, It's working the same as it ever did. I was just playing Risk!! Cannot wait to play Ogre.

Serial: HB4105565
Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: B Board Serial: 1439224 Owner: Ken Page Location: Ipswich / Queensland / Australia
Notes: This is a C64G (Aldi). Although the sticker inside says C64E ???. I bought it from German eBay as I wanted the beautiful cream case with colour toned keyboard and sticker. I also like the bright Green LED. :) It cost me a lot of money to get it shipped to Australia. I tried twice as German sellers refuse to put packing in the box! (Cheap arses!) :( So be warned when buying from Germany. :( I would like another in mint condition if anyone can help. We also try and help preserve Retro Computing here in Australia. Drop in some time! We also have World Wide Retro Party line! ~Ken - Sj of the RCC. http://shazam.zapto.org .-.-.

Serial: DA4113873
Board Version: 250469 Board Revision: REV. 4 Board Serial: 720714 Owner: Arjan Kuiper Location: Oude Pekela, The Netherlands
Notes: This is a genuine 64G with a red LED. When opened up it reveals the following information: Keyboard sticker with the following information: 326166-04, 50880331, MADE IN MALAYSIA. On the board it says PCB ASSY NO.250469, PCB NO.252311 REV 4. There is a sticker on the RF modulator with the following information: P/N 251916-03, 8419. Also there are 2 smaller stickers on the cartridge port with the following information: QC - ASSEMBLED IN HONG KONG - PASSED, BURN IN. And one larger sticker on the cartridge port which includes the board serial, wich says the following: C64E P, HKC S/N 720714, WW 19/87/88.
